parse:
run {
  one u1 : User // Focus on one user's posts.
  some a1, a2 : Ad, d : Day
  a1.date = d
  a2.date = d
  u1.posts = a1 + a2 // u1 posts only Ads.
  all u : User - u1 | no u.posts // Other users post nothing.
  CheckRequirement
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  some u1, u2 : User, a1 : Ad, a2 : Ad, p1 : Photo - Ad, d : Day
  u1 != u2
  a1.date = d
  a2.date = d
  p1.date = d

  u1.posts = a1 + a2 // u1 posts only Ads (satisfies).
  u2.posts = p1      // u2 posts only non-Ads (premise is false, so satisfies).
  CheckRequirement
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  some u1 : User, a1 : Ad, p1 : Photo - Ad, d : Day
  a1.date = d
  p1.date = d
  u1.posts = a1 + p1 // u1 posts an Ad (a1) and a non-Ad (p1), violating the rule.
  CheckRequirement
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  some u1, u2 : User, a1 : Ad, p1 : Photo - Ad, d : Day
  u1 != u2
  a1.date = d
  p1.date = d

  u1.posts = a1 + p1 // u1 violates the rule.
  u2.posts = a1      // u2 satisfies the rule (posts only an Ad).
  CheckRequirement
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  some i1 : Influencer, a1 : Ad, p1 : Photo - Ad, d : Day
  a1.date = d
  p1.date = d
  i1.posts = a1 + p1 // Influencer i1 posts an Ad and a non-Ad.
  CheckRequirement
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  one u : User, one a : Ad, one p : Photo - Ad, one d : Day
  a.date = d
  p.date = d
  u.posts = a + p // The single user 'u' posts an Ad 'a' and a non-Ad 'p', violating the rule.
  CheckRequirement
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  #Influencer = 1
  #Day = 2
  #Photo = 1
  one i : Influencer
  one d0, d1 : Day | d0 != d1
  one p : Photo
  p.date = d0
  i.posts = p // The influencer only posts this one photo on d0, so not on d1.
  all i_var : Influencer, d_var : Day { some p_var : Photo { p_var.date = d_var and p_var in i_var.posts } }
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  #Influencer = 1
  #User = 2 // One Influencer, one regular User
  #Day = 1
  #Photo = 1
  one u_reg : User - Influencer
  one p : Photo
  one d : Day
  p.date = d
  u_reg.posts = p // The only photo is posted by a regular user.
  all i : Influencer { no p_i : Photo | p_i in i.posts } // Influencers post nothing.
  all i_var : Influencer, d_var : Day { some p_var : Photo { p_var.date = d_var and p_var in i_var.posts } }
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  #Influencer = 1
  #Day = 2
  #Photo = 1
  one i : Influencer
  one d_wrong, d_missing : Day | d_wrong != d_missing
  one p : Photo
  p.date = d_wrong
  i.posts = p // The influencer posts, but only on d_wrong. They miss d_missing.
  all i_var : Influencer, d_var : Day { some p_var : Photo { p_var.date = d_var and p_var in i_var.posts } }
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  User0.follows = User1
  User1.follows = User2
  User2.follows = none
  User3.follows = none

  User0.suggested = User2
  User1.suggested = none
  User2.suggested = none
  User3.suggested = none

  all u: User | u.suggested = (u.follows.follows - u.follows - u)
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  User0.follows = User1
  User1.follows = User0
  User2.follows = User3
  User3.follows = User2

  User0.suggested = none
  User1.suggested = none
  User2.suggested = none
  User3.suggested = none

  all u: User | u.suggested = (u.follows.follows - u.follows - u)
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  User0.follows = User1 + User3
  User1.follows = User2
  User3.follows = User2
  User2.follows = none

  User0.suggested = User2
  User1.suggested = none
  User2.suggested = none
  User3.suggested = none

  all u: User | u.suggested = (u.follows.follows - u.follows - u)
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  User0.follows = User1 + User2
  User1.follows = User2
  User2.follows = User3
  User3.follows = none

  User0.suggested = User3
  User1.suggested = User3
  User2.suggested = none
  User3.suggested = none

  all u: User | u.suggested = (u.follows.follows - u.follows - u)
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  User0.follows = User1 + User2 + User3
  User1.follows = User0 + User2 + User3
  User2.follows = User0 + User1 + User3
  User3.follows = User0 + User1 + User2

  User0.suggested = none
  User1.suggested = none
  User2.suggested = none
  User3.suggested = none

  all u: User | u.suggested = (u.follows.follows - u.follows - u)
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  User0.follows = User1
  User1.follows = User2
  User2.follows = none
  User3.follows = none

  User0.suggested = none // ERROR: Should be User2
  User1.suggested = none
  User2.suggested = none
  User3.suggested = none

  all u: User | u.suggested = (u.follows.follows - u.follows - u)
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  User0.follows = User1 + User2
  User1.follows = User2
  User2.follows = none
  User3.follows = none

  User0.suggested = User2 // ERROR: Should be none (User2 is already followed)
  User1.suggested = none
  User2.suggested = none
  User3.suggested = none

  all u: User | u.suggested = (u.follows.follows - u.follows - u)
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  User0.follows = User1
  User1.follows = User0
  User2.follows = none
  User3.follows = none

  User0.suggested = User0 // ERROR: Cannot suggest self
  User1.suggested = none
  User2.suggested = none
  User3.suggested = none

  all u: User | u.suggested = (u.follows.follows - u.follows - u)
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  User0.follows = User1
  User1.follows = User2
  User2.follows = none
  User3.follows = none

  User0.suggested = User3 // ERROR: Should be User2
  User1.suggested = none
  User2.suggested = none
  User3.suggested = none

  all u: User | u.suggested = (u.follows.follows - u.follows - u)
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  User0.follows = User2
  User1.follows = none
  User2.follows = User1
  User3.follows = none

  User0.suggested = none // ERROR: Should be User1
  User1.suggested = none
  User2.suggested = none
  User3.suggested = none

  all u: User | u.suggested = (u.follows.follows - u.follows - u)
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  UserOnlySeesAdsFromFollowedOrSuggested

  // Setup:
  one u1, u2, u3: User
  one a1, a2: Ad

  u1.sees = a1 + a2
  a1 in u2.posts
  a2 in u3.posts
  u1.follows = u2
  u1.suggested = u3
  u2 != u3
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  UserOnlySeesAdsFromFollowedOrSuggested

  // Setup for violation:
  one u1, u2: User
  one a1: Ad

  u1.sees = a1
  a1 in u2.posts
  u2 not in u1.follows
  u2 not in u1.suggested
  u1 != u2
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  UserOnlySeesAdsFromFollowedOrSuggested

  // Setup:
  one u1, u2, u3: User
  one a1, a2: Ad

  u1.sees = a1 + a2
  a1 in u2.posts
  a2 in u3.posts

  // Make a1 violate:
  u2 not in u1.follows
  u2 not in u1.suggested

  // Make a2 conform:
  u1.follows = u3

  u1 != u2
  u1 != u3
  u2 != u3
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  UserOnlySeesAdsFromFollowedOrSuggested

  // Setup:
  one u1: User
  one inf1: Influencer
  one a1: Ad

  u1.sees = a1
  a1 in inf1.posts
  inf1 not in u1.follows
  inf1 not in u1.suggested
} for 4 expect 0
run { WorkersAreHumanOrRobot } for 4 expect 1
run { WorkersAreHumanOrRobot and some Human and no Robot } for 4 expect 1
run { WorkersAreHumanOrRobot and some Robot and no Human } for 4 expect 1
run { WorkersAreHumanOrRobot and some Human and some Robot } for 4 expect 1
run { WorkersAreHumanOrRobot and no Worker } for 4 expect 1
consistent: run { some Human & Robot } for 4 expect 0
run { no Worker and some Human } for 4 expect 0
run { no Worker and some Robot } for 4 expect 0

parse: run { Requirement } for 4 expect 1
run { Requirement and #Workstation = 1 and #Worker > 1 } for 4 expect 1
run { Requirement and (all ws: Workstation | one ws.workers) } for 4 expect 1
run { Requirement and #Workstation > 1 and #Worker > #Workstation } for 4 expect 1
run { Requirement and some Human and some Robot } for 4 expect 1
run { Requirement } { some ws: Workstation | no ws.workers } for 4 expect 0
run { Requirement } { some w: Worker | no w.~workers } for 4 expect 0
run { Requirement } { some w: Worker | #w.~workers > 1 } for 4 expect 0
run { Requirement } { all ws: Workstation | no ws.workers and some Worker } for 4 expect 0
run { Requirement } { some Workstation and some Worker and all w: Worker | #w.~workers != 1 } for 4 expect 0
run { no Component } for 4 everyComponentInOneWorkstation expect 1
run { one c : Component, one ws : Workstation | c.workstation = ws } for 4 everyComponentInOneWorkstation expect 1
run { some disj c1, c2 : Component, ws1, ws2 : Workstation | c1.workstation = ws1 and c2.workstation = ws2 and ws1 != ws2 } for 4 everyComponentInOneWorkstation expect 1
run { some disj c1, c2 : Component, ws : Workstation | c1.workstation = ws and c2.workstation = ws } for 4 everyComponentInOneWorkstation expect 1
run { some c : Component | no c.workstation } for 4 everyComponentInOneWorkstation expect 0
run { some c : Component | some disj ws1, ws2 : Workstation | ws1 in c.workstation and ws2 in c.workstation } for 4 everyComponentInOneWorkstation expect 0
run { one c : Component | no c.workstation } for 4 everyComponentInOneWorkstation expect 0
run { one c : Component | some disj ws1, ws2 : Workstation | c.workstation = ws1 + ws2 } for 4 everyComponentInOneWorkstation expect 0
run { some Component and (all c : Component | no c.workstation) } for 4 everyComponentInOneWorkstation expect 0

run P5_EmptyAndSegregated {
  NoHumansAndRobotsTogether
  some w1: Workstation | no w1.workers // At least one empty workstation
  some w2: Workstation | some w2.workers & Human and no w2.workers & Robot // At least one workstation with only humans
  some w3: Workstation | some w3.workers & Robot and no w3.workers & Human // At least one workstation with only robots
  some Human
  some Robot
} for 4 expect 1

run N3_ComplexMixed {
  not NoHumansAndRobotsTogether
  some w_mixed: Workstation | some w_mixed.workers & Human and some w_mixed.workers & Robot // At least one mixed workstation
  some w_human: Workstation | some w_human.workers & Human and no w_human.workers & Robot // At least one human-only workstation
  some w_robot: Workstation | some w_robot.workers & Robot and no w_robot.workers & Human // At least one robot-only workstation
  some Human
  some Robot
} for 4 expect 0

run N4_MinimalFailure {
  not NoHumansAndRobotsTogether
  one w: Workstation |
    one h: Human, one r: Robot |
    w.workers = h + r
  no (Workstation - w) // Ensure only one workstation exists
  no (Human - h) // Ensure only one human exists
  no (Robot - r) // Ensure only one robot exists
  no (Worker - (h+r)) // No other workers
} for 4 expect 0

run {
    one c: Component
    one w: Workstation
    one human: Human
    c.workstation = w
    w.workers = human
    c in c.parts // Violation
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  some c : Dangerous & Component
  some ws : c.workstation
  some h : Human
  some r : Robot
  h in ws.workers
  r in ws.workers
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  some c1, c2 : Dangerous & Component | c1 != c2
  some ws1, ws2 : Workstation | ws1 != ws2
  some h1, h2 : Human | h1 != h2
  c1.workstation = ws1
  c2.workstation = ws2
  h1 in ws1.workers
  h2 in ws2.workers
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  some c_human : Dangerous & Component
  some ws_human : Workstation
  some h : Human
  c_human.workstation = ws_human
  h in ws_human.workers

  some c_robot : Dangerous & Component - c_human
  some ws_robot : Workstation - ws_human
  some r : Robot
  c_robot.workstation = ws_robot
  ws_robot.workers = r
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  one t1, t2, t3, t4: Track |
    t1.succs = t2
    no t2.succs
    t3.succs = t4
    no t4.succs
    #Track = 4
    no Entry and no Junction
    (t2+t4) in Exit // These are explicitly Exit tracks
    (Track - (t2+t4)) not in Exit // Others are not Exit
  Exit = {t: Track | no t.succs}
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  one t_no_succ: Track |
    no t_no_succ.succs // t_no_succ has no successor
    t_no_succ not in Exit // but it's not an Exit track
    #Track = 2
    no (Track - t_no_succ)
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  one x: Exit, t1: Track, t2: Track |
    x.succs = t1      // x is Exit but has successor t1
    no t2.succs       // t2 has no successor
    t2 not in Exit    // but t2 is not Exit
    #Track = 3
    no (Track - (x+t1+t2))
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  all t: Track | t in Exit // All tracks are Exit
  one t1, t2: Track |
    t1.succs = t2         // One Exit track (t1) has a successor (t2)
    #Track = 2
    no (Track - (t1+t2))
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  one e: Entry |
    no e.succs // An Entry track has no successor
    e not in Exit // An Entry track is not an Exit track (regardless of disjointness)
    #Track = 1
    no (Track - e)
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  exactly 4 Track
  no Junction // No tracks are junctions
  Track0.succs = Track1
  Track1.succs = Track2
  Track2.succs = Track3
  no Track.signals
  all t: Track | t in Junction <=> (#(t.(Track <: succs)) > 1)
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  exactly 4 Track
  Junction = Track3 // Track3 is the only junction
  Track0.succs = Track3
  Track1.succs = Track3
  Track3.succs = Track2
  no Track2.succs
  no Track.signals
  all t: Track | t in Junction <=> (#(t.(Track <: succs)) > 1)
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  exactly 4 Track
  Junction = Track3 // Track3 is the only junction
  Track0.succs = Track3
  Track1.succs = Track3
  Track2.succs = Track3
  no Track.signals
  all t: Track | t in Junction <=> (#(t.(Track <: succs)) > 1)
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  exactly 4 Track
  Junction = Track0 + Track1 // Track0 and Track1 are junctions
  Track2.succs = Track0 + Track1
  Track3.succs = Track0 + Track1
  no Track0.succs
  no Track1.succs
  no Track.signals
  all t: Track | t in Junction <=> (#(t.(Track <: succs)) > 1)
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  exactly 4 Track
  Junction = Track3 // Track3 is the only junction
  Track0.succs = Track3
  Track1.succs = Track3
  Track2.succs = Track3
  Track3.succs = Track2 // A cycle, T2 is a predecessor of T3, and T3 is a successor of T2
  no Track.signals
  all t: Track | t in Junction <=> (#(t.(Track <: succs)) > 1)
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  exactly 4 Track
  Junction = Track0 // Track0 is a junction
  Track0.succs = Track1
  Track1.succs = Track2
  Track2.succs = Track3
  no Track.signals
  all t: Track | t in Junction <=> (#(t.(Track <: succs)) > 1)
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  exactly 4 Track
  Junction = Track1 // Track1 is a junction
  Track0.succs = Track1
  Track1.succs = Track2
  Track2.succs = Track3
  no Track.signals
  all t: Track | t in Junction <=> (#(t.(Track <: succs)) > 1)
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  exactly 4 Track
  no Junction // No tracks are junctions
  Track0.succs = Track2
  Track1.succs = Track2
  Track2.succs = Track3
  no Track.signals
  all t: Track | t in Junction <=> (#(t.(Track <: succs)) > 1)
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  exactly 4 Track
  no Junction // No tracks are junctions
  Track0.succs = Track3
  Track1.succs = Track3
  Track2.succs = Track3
  no Track.signals
  all t: Track | t in Junction <=> (#(t.(Track <: succs)) > 1)
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  exactly 4 Track
  Junction = Track0 // Track0 is a junction
  Track1.succs = Track0
  Track2.succs = Track3
  Track1.succs = Track3 // T3 has predecessors T1 and T2
  no Track0.succs
  no Track.signals
  all t: Track | t in Junction <=> (#(t.(Track <: succs)) > 1)
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  two Entry
  two Speed
  Entry.first.signals = Speed.first
  Entry.last.signals = Speed.last
  (all e: Entry | some s: e.signals & Speed)
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  one Entry
  one Speed
  one Semaphore
  Entry.signals = Speed + Semaphore
  (all e: Entry | some s: e.signals & Speed)
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  one Entry
  one Junction
  one Exit
  one Speed
  Entry.signals = Speed
  Junction.signals = no Signal // Junctions don't need speed signals
  Exit.signals = no Signal    // Exits don't need speed signals
  (all e: Entry | some s: e.signals & Speed)
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  one Entry
  one Semaphore
  Entry.signals = Semaphore
  no Speed // Ensure no Speed signal exists anywhere for clear failure
  (all e: Entry | some s: e.signals & Speed)
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  two Entry
  let e1 = Entry.first, e2 = Entry.last
  one Speed
  one Semaphore
  e1.signals = Semaphore // This Entry track (e1) has no Speed signal
  e2.signals = Speed     // This Entry track (e2) does have a Speed signal
  (all e: Entry | some s: e.signals & Speed)
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  one Entry
  one Junction
  one Speed
  one Semaphore
  Entry.signals = Semaphore // Entry track has a Semaphore signal but no Speed signal
  Junction.signals = Speed  // Speed signal exists, but only on the Junction track
  (all e: Entry | some s: e.signals & Speed)
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  no t: Track | t.succs & Junction
  some t1, t2: Track - Junction
  some j1: Junction
  t1.succs = t2 // Example: a path not involving junctions
  j1.succs = none // Junction exists but nothing leads to it
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  one t1: Track - Junction
  one j1: Junction
  one s1: Semaphore
  t1.succs = j1
  t1.signals = s1
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  one t1: Track - Junction
  one j1: Junction
  one t2: Track - Junction // Another track as successor, not a junction
  one s1: Semaphore
  t1.succs = j1 + t2 // t1 leads to j1 (junction) and t2 (non-junction)
  t1.signals = s1
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  one t1: Track - Junction
  some j1, j2: Junction
  j1 != j2 // Ensure two distinct junctions
  one s1: Semaphore
  t1.succs = j1 + j2 // t1 leads to both J1 and J2
  t1.signals = s1
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  one t1: Track - Junction
  one j1: Junction
  one sp1: Speed
  t1.succs = j1
  t1.signals = sp1 // t1 has a Speed signal, but no Semaphore
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  some t1, t2: Track - Junction
  some j1, j2: Junction
  j1 != j2
  one s1: Semaphore // For t1
  one sp1: Speed   // For t2, causing violation
  t1.succs = j1
  t1.signals = s1 // t1 complies

  t2.succs = j2
  t2.signals = sp1 // t2 violates the rule
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  one t1: Track - Junction
  one j1: Junction
  one t2: Track - Junction // Another track as successor, not a junction
  t1.succs = j1 + t2 // t1 leads to j1 (junction) and t2 (non-junction)
  t1.signals = none  // t1 has no signals, violating the rule
} for 4 expect 0

run Positive1 {
  some Student
  some Project
  no Professor
  Student.projects = Project // The unique student(s) work on all projects
  Req
} expect 1

run Positive2 {
  some Student
  some Professor
  some Project
  no Professor.projects // Professors do not work on any projects
  all proj: Project | some s: Student | proj in s.projects // All projects have a student worker
  Req
} expect 1

run Positive4 {
  one s: Student
  some p1, p2: Project | p1 != p2 // At least two projects
  no Professor
  s.projects = Project // The single student works on all projects
  Req
} expect 1

run Positive5 {
  some s1, s2: Student | s1 != s2
  some p1, p2: Professor | p1 != p2
  some pr1, pr2: Project | pr1 != pr2
  no Professor.projects // Professors are explicitly not working on any projects
  all pr: Project | some s: Student | pr in s.projects // All projects have at least one student worker
  Req
} expect 1

run Negative3 {
  some prof: Professor
  some p1, p2: Project | p1 != p2
  p1 in prof.projects // Professor works on p1
  no p: Person | p2 in p.projects // No one works on p2
  Req
} expect 0

run Negative5 {
  some s: Student
  some p1, p2: Project | p1 != p2 // At least two projects
  no Professor.projects // Ensure "Only students work on projects" holds, to isolate the second violation
  no p: Person | p1 in p.projects // p1 has no workers
  no p: Person | p2 in p.projects // p2 has no workers
  Req
} expect 0

run {
    one prof: Professor
    one s: Student
    one c: Course
    some g1, g2: Grade | g1 != g2
    prof -> g1 in c.grades
    s -> g2 in c.grades
} for 4 expect 0

run {
    some p1, p2: Professor | p1 != p2
    some c1, c2: Course | c1 != c2
    some g1, g2: Grade | g1 != g2
    p1 -> g1 in c1.grades
    p2 -> g2 in c2.grades
} for 4 expect 0

run {
    one p: Professor
    some c1, c2: Course | c1 != c2
    some g1, g2: Grade | g1 != g2
    p -> g1 in c1.grades
    p -> g2 in c2.grades
} for 4 expect 0

run {
    one p: Professor
    one c: Course
    some g1, g2: Grade | g1 != g2
    p -> g1 in c.grades
    p -> g2 in c.grades
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  one c: Course
  two s: Student
  two g: Grade
  let s1 = Student[0], s2 = Student[1] |
  let g1 = Grade[0], g2 = Grade[1] |
    c.grades = (s1 -> g1) + (s2 -> g2)
  all c_all: Course, s_all: Student | lone (s_all.(c_all.grades))
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  one s: Student
  two c: Course
  two g: Grade
  let c1 = Course[0], c2 = Course[1] |
  let g1 = Grade[0], g2 = Grade[1] |
    c1.grades = s -> g1
    c2.grades = s -> g2
  all c_all: Course, s_all: Student | lone (s_all.(c_all.grades))
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  one c: Course
  two s: Student
  three g: Grade
  let s1 = Student[0], s2 = Student[1] |
  let g1 = Grade[0], g2 = Grade[1], g3 = Grade[2] |
    c.grades = (s1 -> g1) + (s1 -> g2) + (s2 -> g3)
  not (all c_all: Course, s_all: Student | lone (s_all.(c_all.grades)))
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  one s: Student
  two c: Course
  three g: Grade
  let c1 = Course[0], c2 = Course[1] |
  let g1 = Grade[0], g2 = Grade[1], g3 = Grade[2] |
    c1.grades = (s -> g1) + (s -> g2)
    c2.grades = s -> g3
  not (all c_all: Course, s_all: Student | lone (s_all.(c_all.grades)))
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  one s: Student
  one c: Course
  two g: Grade
  some Professor
  some Project
  some OtherCourse: Course - c
  s.enrolled = c + OtherCourse
  Professor.teaches = OtherCourse
  c.projects = Project
  OtherCourse.projects = Project
  let g1 = Grade[0], g2 = Grade[1] |
    c.grades = (s -> g1) + (s -> g2)
  not (all c_all: Course, s_all: Student | lone (s_all.(c_all.grades)))
} for 4 expect 0

run Pos_NoStudentPartnerships {
  #Student = 2
  #Project = 2
  #Grade = 0
  #Course = 0
  #Professor = 0
  let s1 = Student[0], s2 = Student[1] |
  let p1 = Project[0], p2 = Project[1] |
    s1.projects = p1 and
    s2.projects = p2 and
    no s : Student - (s1 + s2) | s.projects != none // Other students (if any) have no projects
  student_no_same_partner_diff_projects
} for 4 expect 1

run Pos_OnePartnershipOneProject {
  #Student = 3
  #Project = 2
  #Grade = 0
  #Course = 0
  #Professor = 0
  let s1 = Student[0], s2 = Student[1], s3 = Student[2] |
  let p1 = Project[0], p2 = Project[1] |
    s1.projects = p1 and
    s2.projects = p1 and
    s3.projects = p2 and
    student_no_same_partner_diff_projects
} for 4 expect 1

run Pos_OneStudentMultipleProjects {
  #Student = 1
  #Project = 2
  #Grade = 0
  #Course = 0
  #Professor = 0
  let s1 = Student[0] |
  let p1 = Project[0], p2 = Project[1] |
    s1.projects = p1 + p2 and
    student_no_same_partner_diff_projects
} for 4 expect 1

  let s1 = Student[0], s2 = Student[1], s3 = Student[2], s4 = Student[3] |

} for 4 expect 1

  #Student = 2
  #Project = 2
  #Grade = 0
  #Course = 0
  #Professor = 0
  let s1 = Student[0], s2 = Student[1] |
  let p1 = Project[0], p2 = Project[1] |
    s1.projects = p1 + p2 and
    s2.projects = p2 and
    student_no_same_partner_diff_projects
} for 4 expect 1

run Neg_SamePartnerDifferentProjects {
  #Student = 2
  #Project = 2
  #Grade = 0
  #Course = 0
  #Professor = 0
  let s1 = Student[0], s2 = Student[1] |
  let p1 = Project[0], p2 = Project[1] |
    s1.projects = p1 + p2 and
    s2.projects = p1 + p2 and
    student_no_same_partner_diff_projects
} for 4 expect 0

run Neg_MoreComplexViolation {
  #Student = 3
  #Project = 2
  #Grade = 0
  #Course = 0
  #Professor = 0
  let s1 = Student[0], s2 = Student[1], s3 = Student[2] |
  let p1 = Project[0], p2 = Project[1] |
    s1.projects = p1 + p2 and
    s2.projects = p1 + p2 and
    s3.projects = p2 and // s3 is only in p2
    student_no_same_partner_diff_projects
} for 4 expect 0

run Neg_TripletViolation {
  #Student = 3
  #Project = 2
  #Grade = 0
  #Course = 0
  #Professor = 0
  let s1 = Student[0], s2 = Student[1], s3 = Student[2] |
  let p1 = Project[0], p2 = Project[1] |
    s1.projects = p1 + p2 and
    s2.projects = p1 + p2 and
    s3.projects = p1 + p2 and
    student_no_same_partner_diff_projects
} for 4 expect 0

run Neg_OneViolatingPairAmongOthers {
  #Student = 4
  #Project = 3 // Need P1, P2, P3
  #Grade = 0
  #Course = 0
  #Professor = 0
  let s1 = Student[0], s2 = Student[1], s3 = Student[2], s4 = Student[3] |
  let p1 = Project[0], p2 = Project[1], p3 = Project[2] |
    s1.projects = p1 + p2 and // S1, S2 violate
    s2.projects = p1 + p2 and
    s3.projects = p3 and // S3, S4 are fine
    s4.projects = p3 and
    student_no_same_partner_diff_projects
} for 4 expect 0

run Neg_NestedViolation {
  #Student = 3
  #Project = 2
  #Grade = 0
  #Course = 0
  #Professor = 0
  let s1 = Student[0], s2 = Student[1], s3 = Student[2] |
  let p1 = Project[0], p2 = Project[1] |
    s1.projects = p1 + p2 and // S1, S2 work on P1 and P2 (violation)
    s2.projects = p1 + p2 and
    s3.projects = p1 and // S1, S3 work only on P1 (allowed for this pair)
    student_no_same_partner_diff_projects
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  one c: Course, p1, p2: Project, s1, s2: Student |
    p1 != p2
    c.projects = p1 + p2
    s1 in c.enrolled
    s2 in c.enrolled
    s1.projects = p1 // s1 works only on p1
    s2.projects = p2 // s2 works only on p2
    c.grades = (s1 -> Grade.first) + (s2 -> Grade.next[Grade.first])
  studentProjectGradeConstraint
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  one c: Course, p: Project, s1, s2: Student |
    s1 != s2
    p in c.projects
    s1 in c.enrolled
    s2 in c.enrolled
    p in s1.projects
    p in s2.projects
    c.grades = (s1 -> Grade.first) + (s2 -> Grade.first)
  studentProjectGradeConstraint
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  one c: Course, p: Project, s1, s2: Student |
    s1 != s2
    p in c.projects
    s1 in c.enrolled
    s2 in c.enrolled
    p in s1.projects
    p in s2.projects
    some g: Grade |
      // Ensure g and Grade.next[g] are distinct and exist within the scope.
      g = Grade.first
      c.grades = (s1 -> g) + (s2 -> Grade.next[g])
  studentProjectGradeConstraint
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  one c1, c2: Course, p1, p2: Project, s1, s2, s3, s4: Student |
    c1 != c2 and p1 != p2 and s1 != s2 and s1 != s3 and s1 != s4 and s2 != s3 and s2 != s4 and s3 != s4

    // Course 1, Project 1: s1 and s2 with grades differing by one
    p1 in c1.projects
    s1 in c1.enrolled and s2 in c1.enrolled
    p1 in s1.projects and p1 in s2.projects
    c1.grades = (s1 -> Grade.first) + (s2 -> Grade.next[Grade.first])

    // Course 2, Project 2: s3 and s4 with identical grades
    p2 in c2.projects
    s3 in c2.enrolled and s4 in c2.enrolled
    p2 in s3.projects and p2 in s4.projects
    c2.grades = (s3 -> Grade.next[Grade.first]) + (s4 -> Grade.next[Grade.first])

    // Ensure no other enrollments or project assignments for these students that could cause issues
    no s: Student - (s1 + s2 + s3 + s4)
    no p: Project - (p1 + p2)
    no c: Course - (c1 + c2)
    s1.projects = p1 and s2.projects = p1
    s3.projects = p2 and s4.projects = p2
    s1 not in c2.enrolled and s2 not in c2.enrolled
    s3 not in c1.enrolled and s4 not in c1.enrolled
} for 4 expect 1

run {
  one c: Course, p: Project, s1, s2: Student |
    s1 != s2
    p in c.projects
    s1 in c.enrolled
    s2 in c.enrolled
    p in s1.projects
    p in s2.projects
    some g: Grade |
      // Ensure g and Grade.next[Grade.next[g]] are distinct and exist.
      g = Grade.first // Use Grade.first to ensure Grade.next[Grade.next[g]] exists within for 4 scope
      c.grades = (s1 -> g) + (s2 -> Grade.next[Grade.next[g]])
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  one c: Course, p: Project, s1, s2, s3: Student |
    s1 != s2 and s1 != s3 and s2 != s3
    p in c.projects
    s1 in c.enrolled and s2 in c.enrolled and s3 in c.enrolled
    p in s1.projects and p in s2.projects and p in s3.projects
    some g: Grade |
      g = Grade.first
      // s1 and s2 grades violate the constraint
      c.grades = (s1 -> g) + (s2 -> Grade.next[Grade.next[g]]) + (s3 -> Grade.next[g])
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  one c: Course, p1, p2: Project, s1, s2, s3, s4: Student |
    p1 != p2
    s1 != s2 and s1 != s3 and s1 != s4 and s2 != s3 and s2 != s4 and s3 != s4

    // Violating pair: s1, s2 on p1 in c
    p1 in c.projects
    s1 in c.enrolled and s2 in c.enrolled
    p1 in s1.projects and p1 in s2.projects
    c.grades = (s1 -> Grade.first) + (s2 -> Grade.next[Grade.next[Grade.first]])

    // Compliant pair: s3, s4 on p2 in c (different project in the same course)
    p2 in c.projects
    s3 in c.enrolled and s4 in c.enrolled
    p2 in s3.projects and p2 in s4.projects
    c.grades = c.grades + (s3 -> Grade.next[Grade.first]) + (s4 -> Grade.next[Grade.next[Grade.first]])
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  one c1, c2: Course, p1, p2: Project, s1, s2, s3, s4: Student |
    c1 != c2 and p1 != p2 and s1 != s2 and s1 != s3 and s1 != s4 and s2 != s3 and s2 != s4 and s3 != s4

    // Violation in c1, p1: s1 and s2 grades differ by two
    p1 in c1.projects
    s1 in c1.enrolled and s2 in c1.enrolled
    p1 in s1.projects and p1 in s2.projects
    c1.grades = (s1 -> Grade.first) + (s2 -> Grade.next[Grade.next[Grade.first]])

    // Compliant pair in c2, p2: s3 and s4 grades differ by one
    p2 in c2.projects
    s3 in c2.enrolled and s4 in c2.enrolled
    p2 in s3.projects and p2 in s4.projects
    c2.grades = (s3 -> Grade.first) + (s4 -> Grade.next[Grade.first])

    // Ensure no cross-project/course assignments for these specific students
    s1.projects = p1 and s2.projects = p1
    s3.projects = p2 and s4.projects = p2
    s1 not in c2.enrolled and s2 not in c2.enrolled
    s3 not in c1.enrolled and s4 not in c1.enrolled
} for 4 expect 0

run {
  one c: Course, p: Project, s1, s2: Student |
    s1 != s2
    p in c.projects
    s1 in c.enrolled
    s2 in c.enrolled
    p in s1.projects
    p in s2.projects
    some g: Grade |
      // Ensure g and Grade.next[Grade.next[Grade.next[g]]] exist.
      g = Grade.first // Use Grade.first to guarantee availability
      c.grades = (s1 -> g) + (s2 -> Grade.next[Grade.next[Grade.next[g]]])
} for 4 expect 0
